sourceTitle: Use AI connectors to access more of your team’s knowledge

questions.question: What shortcut can you use to invoke Notion AI globally, regardless of where you are in the app?

options.text: Ctrl + Shift + N
options.explanation: This shortcut does not open Notion AI.

options.text: Shift + Cmd + J
options.explanation: Shift + Cmd + J is the documented global shortcut for Notion AI.

options.text: Alt + A
options.explanation: Alt + A is not associated with Notion AI.

options.text: Cmd + K
options.explanation: Cmd + K opens the quick find, not Notion AI.

questions.question: How do AI connectors handle user permissions when pulling information from connected tools?

options.text: They ignore permissions and show all data to everyone.
options.explanation: That would be a security risk and is not how connectors work.

options.text: They only show content that the user already has access to in the source app.
options.explanation: AI connectors respect the original permissions, so users only see what they could already see.

options.text: They grant temporary admin rights to the requesting user.
options.explanation: No temporary admin rights are given; permissions stay unchanged.

options.text: They ask the admin for approval each time a query is made.
options.explanation: Approval per query is not part of the connector flow.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a benefit of using AI connectors in the guide?

options.text: Reducing time spent searching for information
options.explanation: The guide mentions this as a benefit.

options.text: Automatically fixing bugs in code
options.explanation: AI connectors help surface information but do not automatically fix bugs.

options.text: Consolidating the tool stack into Notion
options.explanation: Consolidation is highlighted as a benefit.

options.text: Maintaining focus by reducing context switching
options.explanation: Reducing context switching is listed as a benefit.

questions.question: When you ask Notion AI a question that it cannot answer using connected sources, what does it do?

options.text: It silently fails without any feedback.
options.explanation: The guide states the AI informs the user.

options.text: It replies with an error and suggests thought starters based on general knowledge.
options.explanation: If it can’t answer, it informs you and offers thought starters.

options.text: It asks the user to manually grant additional permissions.
options.explanation: Permission issues are not the reason for an unanswered query.

options.text: It redirects the query to a human support agent.
options.explanation: No redirection to human support is mentioned.

questions.question: Which connector would you use to quickly retrieve the status of a JIRA ticket without opening JIRA itself?

options.text: AI Connector for Slack
options.explanation: Slack connector accesses Slack messages, not JIRA tickets.

options.text: AI Connector for Google Drive
options.explanation: Google Drive connector works with Docs, Sheets, Slides.

options.text: AI Connector for JIRA
options.explanation: The JIRA connector pulls ticket data directly into Notion AI.

options.text: AI Connector for Microsoft Teams
options.explanation: Microsoft Teams connector accesses Teams chats and files.

questions.question: How can the Slack AI connector improve the creation of status reports?

options.text: It automatically writes the report in a predefined template without user input.
options.explanation: User still needs to ask for the relevant information.

options.text: It can summarize relevant Slack conversations from a chosen time period and feed them into the report.
options.explanation: Summarizing Slack threads helps populate status reports.

options.text: It replaces the need for any written report by posting updates directly in Slack.
options.explanation: The connector aids report creation, not replacement.

options.text: It syncs Slack messages to a Google Sheet for manual analysis.
options.explanation: Syncing to Google Sheet is not the described function.

questions.question: Which scenario demonstrates a use case for the Google Drive AI connector?

options.text: Pulling the latest JIRA sprint metrics into a Notion page.
options.explanation: JIRA metrics belong to the JIRA connector.

options.text: Extracting key terms from a legal contract stored in Google Docs without leaving Notion.
options.explanation: The connector allows referencing Google Docs content directly.

options.text: Monitoring real‑time Slack channel activity.
options.explanation: Slack activity is handled by the Slack connector.

options.text: Generating a PowerPoint presentation from Notion content automatically.
options.explanation: PowerPoint generation is not a described feature.

questions.question: What advantage does the Microsoft Teams AI connector provide for meeting follow‑ups?

options.text: It automatically sends meeting recordings to every participant’s inbox.
options.explanation: Recording distribution is not mentioned.

options.text: It extracts key decisions from meeting transcripts and drafts action items directly in Notion.
options.explanation: This is the specific benefit highlighted for Teams.

options.text: It replaces the need for a meeting by generating a summary from calendar invites.
options.explanation: Meetings still occur; the connector aids post‑meeting work.

options.text: It syncs Teams chat history into a Google Sheet for analysis.
options.explanation: Syncing to Google Sheets is not part of the Teams connector feature set.

questions.question: According to the guide, what is the estimated time saved per question when using AI connectors compared to manual searching?

options.text: Up to 5 minutes
options.explanation: The guide cites a larger saving.

options.text: Up to 10 minutes
options.explanation: The guide states up to 10 minutes saved per question.

options.text: Up to 30 minutes
options.explanation: 30 minutes is far beyond the stated figure.

options.text: No measurable time saved; it's purely for convenience.
options.explanation: The guide provides a specific time‑saving estimate.

questions.question: If a new hire asks Notion AI for information about the company's HR policy, which sources might the AI draw from according to the article?

options.text: Only the HR database within Notion.
options.explanation: The AI also pulls from connected tools.

options.text: Both the HR database and relevant Slack conversations.
options.explanation: The article mentions combining HR database and Slack discussions.

options.text: External public websites about HR best practices.
options.explanation: External web sources are not used unless explicitly connected.

options.text: Only the latest Google Docs titled “HR Policy”.
options.explanation: Google Docs would be used only if the Google Drive connector is connected; the example focuses on HR DB and Slack.
